item.title: LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Release Date & All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Batman has been a staple for LEGO video games since the PS2 era, with the original being a beloved classic to many. Now, almost 20 years after the first LEGO Batman was released, it’s time for LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight.

Get ready for an all-encompassing adventure that explores and combines the rich history of the Dark Knight. With its own unique story that pulls elements from every Batman avenue, be it comics, films or games, it’s looking like LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will be a must-play for any Batman fan out there.

If that’s you, then you’ll want to know when the game is set to release, as well as which platforms it’ll be playable on; luckily for you, we’ve got all the information you’ll need.

When Does LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Come Out? Release Date, Explained

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight is set to launch on May 22, 2026.

LEGO games have never really had a reliable release window, with past titles launched throughout the year. That being said, TT Games’ last venture with LEGO Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga was released in April, so the springtime release could become consistent.

  • More Insider Gaming: LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Updated PC System Requirements Revealed

Regardless, that means it’s almost time to don the cowl and take to the streets of Gotham. With six chapters of missions and an open world to explore, there should be plenty to keep you occupied, but what time does the game launch?

LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Exact Start Times

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will officially launch at 10 AM PT on May 22, 2026.

If you’re on the West Coast, that gives you practically all day to play as the Caped Crusader, though it’s a different story for those of you across the world. Here’s a handy time zone converter to help you figure out when you can jump into LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight for the first time:

Can You Play LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight On PlayStation? PS5 Status, Explained

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will be a day-one release on the PlayStation 5, though it won’t be available on past-gen platforms.

  • More Insider Gaming: Sony PlayStation Lawsuit Refund: How To Check Eligibility and Claim Your Money

This marks LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight as the first mainline LEGO game to be exclusive to current-gen platforms, with the Skywalker Saga being available on the PS4. And given the nature and history of LEGO games, it was always going to appear on the PS5.

Can You Play LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight On Xbox? Xbox Series X|S Status, Explained

The same can be said for the Xbox, as LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will also launch day one on Xbox Series X|S.

And just like the PlayStation version, it won’t be playable on past iterations of the Xbox. In fairness to TT Games, it’s about time we move away from past-gen consoles, and it looks like LEGO Batman is taking that leap.

Can You Play LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight On PC?

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight is also coming to PC, meaning it’s not a console exclusive.

With GTA 6 buckling down on a console-first release plan, some LEGO Batman fans might have worried that TT Games might take a similar approach. But fear not, as that’s not the case, and it will be purchasable on both Steam and the Epic Games Store.

Can You Play LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight On Nintendo Switch? Nintendo Switch 2 Status, Explained

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will be coming to the Nintendo Switch 2, but not alongside other platforms.

Nintendo fans, you’ll have to wait a bit longer, as there is currently no launch date for the Nintendo Switch 2 version. It’s still slated for a 2026 release, but as things stand, it’s unclear when it could be.

  • More Insider Gaming: May 2026 Video Game Releases: Full Schedule and Biggest Games

It’s not the only game to struggle with a Switch 2 port, as 007 First Light has also had its Switch 2 version delayed to late Summer. Given that both games release around the same time, could LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight launch on the Switch 2 in late Summer as well? It’s all speculation for now, but hang in there, Switch fans—it’s definitely on the way.

Are you excited to finally play LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

item.title: Pixelated Zelda-like Mina the Hollower lands its "final" release date
item.content:encoded:
After a bit of a delay, Shovel Knight developer Yacht Club Games has announced its new - and "final" - release date for Mina the Hollower.

Mina the Hollower will release across Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch 2, PC, Mac, Linux, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X/S later this month, on 29th May. It will retail for $19.99 (I have emailed for clarification on UK pricing).

"Prepare yourself for one of the greatest top-down adventures ever to be delivered directly to your soul for less than 20 bucks," Yacht Club Games proclaimed on social media platform X, announcing Mina the Hollower's release date. In the meantime, those who are keen to give Mina the Hollower a go on release can wishlist the game now.

In a first for developer Yacht Club Games, Mina the Hollower has nothing to do with Shovel Knight. Rather, this time players will have the chance to take on the mantle of adorable but feisty mouse Mina as she sets out to save a cursed island from the perils that have befallen it. It has been one of my most anticipated games since it was first announced back in 2022 (it was actually one of my first articles on Eurogamer).

Hopefully, all will go well on Mina's release, as back in December, it was reported a lot was riding on the game's success, with Yacht Club Games calling it a "make-or-break" moment for the company. At the time, studio founder Sean Velasco said if Mina the Hollower were to "flop", then Yacht Club Games would still exist, but it would require some kind of cash injection fast.

I played the demo for Mina the Hollower on Switch 2 last year, and loved it. "I collected Bonestone, I popped off various goblins and bats, I burrowed my way underneath enemies to spring up excitingly from behind and I negotiated perilous pits, all while bopping along to a gloriously retro-infused soundtrack… Oh yes, I am certainly looking forward to Mina the Hollower's full release," I wrote back in August. This feeling hasn't changed.

item.title: Pragmata Has Hit A New Sales Milestone In Just 16 Days
item.content:encoded:
That's how we say "good job"!

Capcom's brand-new IP Pragmata has gotten off to an excellent start, with the game now surpassing 2 million copies sold in just 16 days.

Originally launched on 17th April 2026, Pragmata managed to shift over 1 million copies in just 2 days. The Switch 2 version saw a slightly later launch in Japan, landing on 24th April. It received critical acclaim across the board (along with some predictably irritating online discourse), which has no doubt helped to fuel some of those early sales.

Just recently, Capcom's CEO in the US, Rob Dyer, hinted that Pragmata may just be the start of something special, indicating that the company has a new IP that "we can continue to go down".

Meanwhile, Capcom's other major launch of 2026, Resident Evil Requiem, has recently surpassed 7 million copies sold. Naturally, the gulf here is due to RE's pedigree in the games industry, with Requiem serving as the latest mainline title in the series.

We here at Nintendo Life awarded Pragmata a score of 9/10, stating that it's "as another excellent Switch 2 port from Capcom, and hopefully mark the beginning of a new blockbuster franchise".

Have you picked up Pragmata yet? What do you make of these latest sales figures? Let us know with a comment.

[source capcom.co.jp]
